sections.paragraph:
unused in Kal; prop. to surround, i.q. עָטַר, see a long series of connected roots under the word אָזַר.

Piel-

(1) to surround, to environ, in a hostile sense, Jud. 20:43 Psa 22:13.

(2) to wait, as in Syriac and Chaldee, prob. from the idea of going round; comp. חוּל No. 7, and יָחַל Job 36:2.

Hiphil-

(1) i.q. Piel No. 1, in a hostile sense, Hab 1:4 but in a good sense, Psa. 142:8 , followed by בְּ.

(2) to put on as a crown, to crown oneself with anything (see כֶּתֶר ). Pro 14:18, יַכְתִּירוּ דַעַת עֲרוּמִים “the prudent are crowned with knowledge.”

Hence כֹּתֶרֶת, and-
